mnemonics.title: Flynn's Taxonomy 2x2 Grid
mnemonics.content: Rows = Instruction streams (Single/Multiple), Columns = Data streams (Single/Multiple). SISD (top-left, sequential), SIMD (top-right, GPU), MISD (bottom-left, rare), MIMD (bottom-right, most common).

mnemonics.title: MESI Protocol States
mnemonics.content: My Exclusive Shared Invalid -> Modified (My dirty data), Exclusive (My clean data, only copy), Shared (Our clean data, multiple copies), Invalid (No valid data here).

mnemonics.title: Amdahl's Law Key Insight
mnemonics.content: Speedup = 1/s (maximum with infinite processors). s = serial fraction. 10% serial = max 10x speedup. 1% serial = max 100x speedup. The serial fraction is the bottleneck. Think: 'Serial Sets the Speed limit'.

mnemonics.title: Why Multi-core? The Three Walls
mnemonics.content: Power Wall (too much heat), Memory Wall (memory too slow), ILP Wall (no more instruction-level parallelism to exploit). Solution: go multi-core and parallel. Think: walls blocked single-core, so we went wide instead of tall.

mnemonics.title: Parallel Challenges: R.L.S.C.
mnemonics.content: Race conditions, Load balancing, Synchronization, Communication overhead. These four challenges make parallel programming harder than sequential. Remember: Running in parallel Looks Simple but Creates headaches.
